pattern: Hashing each row as an immutable tuple lets every column be matched against all rows in aggregate, counting equal sequences by multiplicity rather than comparing every pair.
hints: A row and a column are equal only if they are identical ordered sequences, so a sequence can be used as a hash key.
Tally how many rows equal each sequence, then for each column add the count of matching rows.
Build a frequency map of rows (as tuples); for every column form its tuple and add the map's count for it.

edge_inputs.description: duplicate rows and columns multiply matches
identity-like grid with no equal pair
symmetric grid pairs every row with its column

stress.description: random square grid with small value range to force collisions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 200))
    grid = [[rng.randint(1, 3) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: tiny value range creates many equal rows and columns
